title: Artist name doesn't respect custom branding style
description: maygrabli presents her name as a single lowercase word — a deliberate branding choice. The platform should let artists control how their name is displayed (casing, spacing, punctuation) rather than auto-formatting it. Other artists may have similar stylized names.

title: Calendar view is broken for year-only exhibitions
description: When an exhibition only has a year (no specific start/end month), the calendar view renders it as a bar spanning all 12 months — implying a year-long show, which is wrong. On top of that, each year gets its own full 12-column calendar row, so a spread like 2025 / 2022 / 2015 creates three massive mostly-empty rows. The calendar format only makes sense with precise dates; for year-only data, it should either fall back to the timeline view or show a much more compact year-based layout.

title: Contact form topic dropdown doesn't scale with large catalogs
description: The 'Topic' dropdown in the contact form lists every artwork individually as an inquiry option (e.g. 'Inquiry about Askadinya', 'Inquiry about Palestinian Breakfast'). An artist with 50+ works would have an unusable dropdown. A searchable input or autocomplete field would handle large catalogs far better.

title: Language switch is slow — static content should be pre-translated
description: Switching language via the language button has a noticeable delay. Since profile content (bio, artwork titles, exhibition names) is static, translations should be generated once and cached — not computed on every toggle. The translation quality itself looks good.

title: Artwork status labels use inconsistent font
description: The status labels on art pieces (e.g. 'Available', 'Sold') appear in a different font than the rest of the platform, breaking visual consistency.

title: "main" label appears on artwork image hover
description: When hovering over an artwork image on the detail page, a 'main' label appears in the bottom-right corner of the image. This looks like an internal branch name or debug tag leaking into the UI — not intentional.

title: Selecting a frame changes the popup size
description: In the 'Place Artwork' mockup popup, selecting a frame option causes the entire popup to resize. The modal dimensions should stay fixed regardless of which controls are active.
